lead_profile_text: STL Switch — Rational Evaluator. First investment property owned via limited company with wife. Currently receiving LTL offers of £1,700/mo unfurnished. Evaluating whether higher STL income justifies full furnishing investment. Undecided — acknowledges income case but calls it 'possibly an excuse not to do the harder thing'.

top_objections.text: Setup cost and effort barrier — property is unfurnished and requires full investment
top_objections.zac_response: Furnishing price quote to be sent to make investment case clear

top_objections.text: Income instability vs £1,700/mo guaranteed LTL offer
top_objections.zac_response: STL net £2,017/mo vs LTL net £1,550/mo — significant material advantage of £467/mo

top_questions.text: How does the STL net compare to the LTL offers I'm receiving?
top_questions.answer: STL gross ~£41,000/yr, net ~£24,205/yr (£2,017/mo) vs LTL £1,700/mo (~£1,550 net after agent). STL advantage ~£467/mo

top_questions.text: How does the limited company structure affect the STL option?
top_questions.answer: Ltd company structure suits STL tax treatment

what_moved_them: STL net materially higher (£2,017 vs £1,550 net)
Ltd company structure suits STL tax treatment
what_fell_flat: Setup cost and furnishing effort — LTL is the simpler path

conversion_gaps.gap: Full furnishing cost not yet quantified
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Send furnishing price estimate to make the investment payback case clear

conversion_gaps.gap: Income instability perception vs guaranteed LTL offer
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Provide income report with historical occupancy/performance data
